questions.prompt: Which property says 'no two inputs share an output'?
questions.explanation: Injective (one-to-one): if $x_1 \neq x_2$ then $f(x_1) \neq f(x_2)$. Equivalently, two arrows never land on the same output.

questions.prompt: Which property says 'every output is reached by at least one input'?
hints: Look at the OUTPUT side: every element has at least one incoming arrow.
questions.explanation: Surjective (onto): for every $y$ in the codomain, there exists at least one $x$ with $f(x) = y$. No element in the output set is 'left out'.

choices: No — it is not injective because $f(2) = f(-2)$.
No — it is not surjective because $x^3$ skips negative values.
Yes — it is both injective and surjective.
hints: Try plotting $y = x^3$. Does any horizontal line cross it more than once?
Does any horizontal line miss it entirely?
questions.explanation: Cubing is strictly increasing on $\mathbb{R}$, so it's injective. And it covers all of $\mathbb{R}$ (cube roots exist for negatives), so it's surjective. Hence bijective — with inverse $f^{-1}(y) = \sqrt[3]{y}$.

questions.promptTex: f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}, \ f(x) = e^x. \text{ Which is correct?}
hints: $e^x$ is strictly increasing — does any horizontal line cross it twice?
What values does $e^x$ output? Can it ever equal 0 or be negative?
questions.explanation: $e^x$ is strictly increasing, so injective. But it always outputs a positive number, so it never reaches $\leq 0$ — not surjective onto $\mathbb{R}$. To make it bijective, restrict the codomain to $(0, \infty)$; then the inverse is $\ln$.

questions.prompt: A function has an inverse if and only if it is:
hints: To run the function backwards, each output must have one and only one input.
'Exactly one input per output' = injective AND surjective.
questions.explanation: Bijectivity is exactly the condition under which the reverse mapping is well-defined and total: each output has exactly one input. Without injectivity, the inverse is ambiguous; without surjectivity (onto the chosen codomain), the inverse is undefined on some outputs.
